blocks.content: Thursday. The steps. Same time as always — after school, right before the late buses. The crowd thinned out fast and then it was just the ones who were always there.
Crystal came down the steps and sat two spots to my left. Close enough to talk, far enough to say we were just sitting in the same place.
That was the whole greeting. We'd gotten past the performance of it.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: How was your week so far.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He's around. But it's different now. He's thinking.
She looked out at the street. There was a pause — not awkward. The kind you don't have to fill.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Can I ask you something kind of personal?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Why football? Like, what is it actually. Not the answer you give to people who don't play.
